<|channel|>analysis<|message|>We need to analyze the transcript to determine which player is likely a Werewolf. We have four players: Justin, Mike, James, Mitchell. We need to deduce roles based on actions described.

We have a transcript of a game session. Let's parse the transcript.

The transcript seems messy; many lines are unclear. Let's try to interpret.

First, the announcer says "Everyone, close your eyes." Then "Mitchell: Fucking hell." Then "Announcer: Werewolves-" etc. It seems the announcer is giving instructions for the night. The transcript includes the night actions: Seer wakes up, Robber wakes up, Troublemaker wakes up, Insomniac wakes up. The transcript includes some comments about who did what.

We need to figure out which player ended up as a Werewolf. We have to consider that roles can swap. The transcript includes some statements about "I was insomniac" etc.

Let's parse the transcript step by step.

Lines:

[1] Announcer: Everyone, close your eyes.
[2] Mitchell: Fucking hell.
[3] Announcer: Werewolves-
[4] Mitchell: Werewolves.
[5] Announcer: ...wake up and look for other werewolves.
[6] Justin: What's that game called? Atmosphere?
[7] James: Yep.
[8] Announcer: If there is one werewolf, you may look at a card from the center. Werewolves, close your eyes. Seer, wake up. You may look at another player's card or two of the center cards. Seer, close your eyes. Robber, wake up. You may exchange your card with another player's card and then view your new card. Robber, close your eyes. Troublemaker, wake up. You may exchange cards between two other players. Troublemaker, close your eyes. Insomniac, wake up and look at your card. Insomniac, close your eyes. Everyone keep your eyes closed and reach out and move your card around slightly.

So the night actions: Seer, Robber, Troublemaker, Insomniac. The transcript includes some comments about "I was insomniac" etc.

Then [9] James: Nailed it.
[10] Announcer: Everyone, wake up.
[11] James: Pop sucked. Pop was by far the worst.
[12] Mike: I don't know. I kind of like-
[13] Mitchell: Troublemaker, you switched those two.
[14] Justin: Which two?
[15] Mitchell: Those two.
[16] Justin: Why do you say that?
[17] Mitchell: I was insomniac, and I woke up exactly how I went to sleep.
[18] Mike: You switched us two?
[19] Mitchell: Yeah. Unless he's-
[20] Mike: Unless he's Tanner.
[21] Mitchell: Unless... All right. Unless he's a werewolf, and he saw troublemaker.
[22] Mike: It's possible too.
[23] Justin: Hashtag slambulance.
[24] Mitchell: Hashtag slambulance.
[25] Mike: What'd you look at?
[26] Mitchell: So whose stories can you confirm?
[27] Mike: None.
[28] James: Nobody's, but I can tell you that's off the mat.
[29] Mitchell: The what?
[30] James: I can tell you that's off the mat.
[31] Mike: Yeah. That's what he saw.
[32] James: That's what I saw as a seer.
[33] Mitchell: So far, everything could be adding up.
[34] Mike: So you switched us two?
[35] Mitchell: I should have asked him. Fuck. Instead of just saying, I should have asked, I could have confirmed it.
[36] Mike: What?
[37] Mitchell: I should asked him who he switched because I know I wasn't switched as an insomniac. Now what's possible is he was a werewolf. He saw a troublemaker. Now he's just calling it.
[38] Justin: ... (some confusion)
[39] Mitchell: Oh.
[40] Mike: I vote for myself.
[41] James: I'm going to say vote to the left. There's two werewolves in the center. I can confirm there's at least one, and no one was robber or burgled.
[42] Justin: And you woke up as the insomniac?
[43] Mitchell: Yeah.
[44] Justin: If I find out one of you guys is lying...
[45] Mitchell: Can we abstain...
[46] James: Can we vote center?
[47] Mitchell: Because if we always vote to the left, and let's say there's a tanner...
[48] Mike: He couldn't vote for himself.
[49] Mitchell: If there was a werewolf ... No. Oh, no, no. Right. Nothing breaks.
[50] Mike: Nothing breaks.
[51] Mitchell: Nothing breaks.
[52] Mike: I mean, this is the tanner could be
[53] Mitchell: [inaudible 00:03:22].
[54] Mike: ...to be a bit of a douche could vote not to the left.
[55] James: And just lose to everybody.
[56] Mitchell: Right
[57] Mike: But get somebody.
[58] Justin: If no player receives more than one vote, no one dies. One way the players might achieve this is if everyone votes the same direction around the table with each player receiving one vote.
[59] Mitchell: Right.
[60] Justin: That means nobody dies.
[61] James: Vote to the left.
[62] Justin: Vote to the left? Do you guys want to get on that boat?
[63] James: Yeah.
[64] Mitchell: Vote now.
[65] Justin: Vote now. I think a werewolf-
[66] Announcer: Time is up everyone.
[67] Justin: ...would have started talking a lot earlier.

<|channel|>analysis<|message|>We need to analyze the transcript to determine which player is likely a Werewolf. We have four players: Justin, Mike, James, Mitchell. We need to deduce roles based on actions described.

We have a transcript of a game session. Let's parse the transcript.

The transcript seems messy; many lines are unclear. Let's try to interpret.

First, the announcer says "Everyone, close your eyes." Then "Mitchell: Fucking hell." Then "Announcer: Werewolves-" etc. It seems the announcer is giving instructions for the night. The transcript includes the night actions: Seer wakes up, Robber wakes up, Troublemaker wakes up, Insomniac wakes up. The transcript includes some comments about who did what.

We need to figure out which player ended up as a Werewolf. We have to consider that roles can swap. The transcript includes some statements about "I was insomniac" etc.

Let's parse the transcript step by step.

Lines:

[1] Announcer: Everyone, close your eyes.
[2] Mitchell: Fucking hell.
[3] Announcer: Werewolves-
[4] Mitchell: Werewolves.
[5] Announcer: ...wake up and look for other werewolves.
[6] Justin: What's that game called? Atmosphere?
[7] James: Yep.
[8] Announcer: If there is one werewolf, you may look at a card from the center. Werewolves, close your eyes. Seer, wake up. You may look at another player's card or two of the center cards. Seer, close your eyes. Robber, wake up. You may exchange your card with another player's card and then view your new card. Robber, close your eyes. Troublemaker, wake up. You may exchange cards between two other players. Troublemaker, close your eyes. Insomniac, wake up and look at your card. Insomniac, close your eyes. Everyone keep your eyes closed and reach out and move your card around slightly.

So the night actions: Seer, Robber, Troublemaker, Insomniac. The transcript includes some comments about "I was insomniac" etc.

Then [9] James: Nailed it.
[10] Announcer: Everyone, wake up.
[11] James: Pop sucked. Pop was by far the worst.
[12] Mike: I don't know. I kind of like-
[13] Mitchell: Troublemaker, you switched those two.
[14] Justin: Which two?
[15] Mitchell: Those two.
[16] Justin: Why do you say that?
[17] Mitchell: I was insomniac, and I woke up exactly how I went to sleep.
[18] Mike: You switched us two?
[19] Mitchell: Yeah. Unless he's-
[20] Mike: Unless he's Tanner.
[21] Mitchell: Unless... All right. Unless he's a werewolf, and he saw troublemaker.
[22] Mike: It's possible too.
[23] Justin: Hashtag slambulance.
[24] Mitchell: Hashtag slambulance.
[25] Mike: What'd you look at?
[26] Mitchell: So whose stories can you confirm?
[27] Mike: None.
[28] James: Nobody's, but I can tell you that's off the mat.
[29] Mitchell: The what?
[30] James: I can tell you that's off the mat.
[31] Mike: Yeah. That's what he saw.
[32] James: That's what I saw as a seer.
[33] Mitchell: So far, everything could be adding up.
[34] Mike: So you switched us two?
[35] Mitchell: I should have asked him. Fuck. Instead of just saying, I should have asked, I could have confirmed it.
[36] Mike: What?
[37] Mitchell: I should asked him who he switched because I know I wasn't switched as an insomniac. Now what's possible is he was a werewolf. He saw a troublemaker. Now he's just calling it.
[38] Justin: ... (some confusion)
[39] Mitchell: Oh.
[40] Mike: I vote for myself.
[41] James: I'm going to say vote to the left. There's two werewolves in the center. I can confirm there's at least one, and no one was robber or burgled.
[42] Justin: And you woke up as the insomniac?
[43] Mitchell: Yeah.
[44] Justin: If I find out one of you guys is lying...
[45] Mitchell: Can we abstain...
[46] James: Can we vote center?
[47] Mitchell: Because if we always vote to the left, and let's say there's a tanner...
[48] Mike: He couldn't vote for himself.
[49] Mitchell: If there was a werewolf ... No. Oh, no, no. Right. Nothing breaks.
[50] Mike: Nothing breaks.
[51] Mitchell: Nothing breaks.
[52] Mike: I mean, this is the tanner could be
[53] Mitchell: [inaudible 00:03:22].
[54] Mike: ...to be a bit of a douche could vote not to the left.
[55] James: And just lose to everybody.
[56] Mitchell: Right
[57] Mike: But get somebody.
[58] Justin: If no player receives more than one vote, no one dies. One way the players might achieve this is if everyone votes the same direction around the table with each player receiving one vote.
[59] Mitchell: Right.
[60] Justin: That means nobody dies.
[61] James: Vote to the left.
[62] Justin: Vote to the left? Do you guys want to get on that boat?
[63] James: Yeah.
[64] Mitchell: Vote now.
[65] Justin: Vote now. I think a werewolf-
[66] Announcer: Time is up everyone.
[67] Justin: ...would have started talking a lot earlier.
